prompt: Using the provided reference image as a Labanotation-style dance score, interpret the 4-beat movement phrase and convert it into a pose-control character sheet. Create exactly 4 full-body panels arranged in a clean 2×2 grid on a plain white studio background, showing the same character performing the pose for each beat. Add bold Japanese beat labels in the upper-left of each panel: "1拍目", "2拍目", "3拍目", and "4拍目". Use a photorealistic young female model wearing a white school-uniform blazer, black bow tie, gray plaid skirt, white socks, and black Mary Jane shoes, with black twin tails. Translate the notation into these four poses: beat 1: standing on one leg with one arm raised straight upward and the opposite leg bent back; beat 2: one arm raised upward, body angled slightly, one knee lifted and crossed in front; beat 3: standing front-facing with both arms extended horizontally to the sides; beat 4: standing front-facing with both hands held together near the chest. Keep the character consistent across all four panels, use soft even lighting, minimal shadows, and a simple catalog-like dance reference layout.
